title: Load tipper firmware from a USB drive using HMI boot mode
summary: Install a new firmware version on the tipper controller by powering the tipper down, entering HMI boot mode by holding the top three HMI buttons during power-up, loading firmware from a USB drive, then rebooting normally and verifying the HMI returns to the expected post-boot screens.
when_to_use: Use when a new firmware version must be installed on the tipper controller from a USB drive and the controller must be returned to normal boot operation with the expected HMI screens visible afterward.

responsibilities: Power the tipper off and on using the disconnect
Enter HMI boot mode using the top three HMI buttons
Install firmware from the USB drive
Verify normal boot and expected HMI screens after reboot

safety_notes: This procedure is not marked support-safe in the source candidate.
The procedure includes powering the tipper off and on with the disconnect.
Do not continue if the controller does not enter the expected gray-screen mode or does not return to normal boot behavior after firmware loading.
access_or_tools_needed: USB drive containing the new firmware version

system_preconditions: USB drive contains the new firmware version
USB dongle is available on the HMI

visual_references.description: HMI-related artifact associated with holding the top three buttons during power-up and waiting for the gray screen.
HMI-related artifact associated with startup screen navigation and gray-screen entry sequence.
HMI screenshot showing expected normal boot state with the Visu_Default screen and IP/subnet mask display.
HMI screenshot related to expected post-boot controller startup and network verification state.
HMI screenshot associated with navigation to the Visu_MCP_Dual screen using F3.
Artifact associated with the Visu_MCP_Dual screen referenced for F3 navigation.
Artifact associated with the Visu_MCP_Dual screen referenced for F3 navigation.
System screen artifact related to stopping/startup procedures and power state context.

steps.title: Turn off power to the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the tipper in the required powered-down state before entering firmware load mode.
steps.instruction: Turn off power to the tipper with the disconnect.
steps.healthy_condition: Power to the tipper is off and the system is ready for the startup button sequence.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper cannot be powered off with the disconnect or its power state is unclear.

actions.label: Power off tipper
actions.exact_action: Turn off power to the tipper with the disconnect.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: General system power-state and stopping/startup screen context related to powering equipment off.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tipper cannot be powered off with the disconnect.

source_evidence.text: Turn off power to the tipper with the disconnect.

steps.title: Press and hold the top three HMI buttons
steps.purpose: Begin the button sequence required to enter the firmware load mode during startup.
steps.instruction: Press and hold the top three buttons on the HMI.
steps.expected_result: The top three HMI buttons are held continuously in preparation for power-up.
steps.healthy_condition: The buttons remain held before and during the next power-on step.
steps.failure_condition: The buttons cannot be held or the sequence cannot be started as described.

actions.label: Hold top three buttons
actions.exact_action: Press and hold the top three buttons on the HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: HMI area associated with the startup button sequence and gray-screen entry.
HMI setup/startup screen context related to the top-button hold sequence.

stop_or_escalate_if: The top three HMI buttons cannot be identified or held as required.

source_evidence.text: Press and hold the top three buttons on the HMI.

steps.title: Power on while continuing to hold the buttons
steps.purpose: Use the required startup sequence to enter the special firmware load mode.
steps.instruction: Continue holding the top three HMI buttons while powering on the tipper.
steps.expected_result: The tipper powers on while the top three HMI buttons remain held.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper powers on and the button hold is maintained through startup.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper does not power on correctly or the button hold sequence is interrupted.

actions.label: Power on tipper during button hold
actions.exact_action: Continue holding the top three HMI buttons while powering on the tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Startup sequence context showing continued button hold during power-on.
Startup/setup screen context associated with entering the gray-screen mode.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tipper does not power on as expected during the button-hold sequence.

source_evidence.text: Continue holding the top three HMI buttons while powering on the tipper.

steps.title: Wait for the gray screen
steps.purpose: Confirm that the controller has entered the expected firmware load mode.
steps.instruction: Keep holding the buttons until a gray screen appears on the HMI.
steps.expected_result: A gray screen appears on the HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The HMI displays the gray screen while following the startup button sequence.
steps.failure_condition: The gray screen does not appear.

actions.label: Confirm gray screen
actions.exact_action: Keep holding the buttons until a gray screen appears on the HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Gray-screen entry context on the HMI during startup.
Startup/setup screen context associated with the gray screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The gray screen does not appear while holding the top three HMI buttons during power-up.

source_evidence.text: Keep holding the buttons until a gray screen appears on the HMI.
If the gray screen does not appear while holding the top three HMI buttons during power-up, stop and escalate.

steps.title: Insert the firmware USB drive
steps.purpose: Make the firmware file available to the HMI for installation.
steps.instruction: Plug the USB drive containing the new firmware version into the USB dongle on the HMI.
steps.expected_result: The USB drive is connected to the HMI USB dongle.
steps.healthy_condition: The firmware USB drive is inserted and available for selection.
steps.failure_condition: The USB drive cannot be connected or is not available to the HMI.

actions.label: Insert USB drive
actions.exact_action: Plug the USB drive containing the new firmware version into the USB dongle on the HMI.

stop_or_escalate_if: The USB drive containing the new firmware version cannot be connected to the HMI USB dongle.

source_evidence.text: Plug the USB drive containing the new firmware version into the USB dongle on the HMI.

steps.title: Select install from file
steps.purpose: Start the firmware installation workflow from the HMI.
steps.instruction: Press the INSTALL FROM FILE button.
steps.expected_result: The HMI begins the file-based firmware installation process.
steps.healthy_condition: The INSTALL FROM FILE control is accepted and the system proceeds to firmware loading.
steps.failure_condition: The INSTALL FROM FILE action cannot be started.

actions.label: Press INSTALL FROM FILE
actions.exact_action: Press the INSTALL FROM FILE button.

stop_or_escalate_if: The firmware cannot be loaded from the USB drive.

source_evidence.text: Press the INSTALL FROM FILE button.
If the firmware cannot be loaded from the USB drive, stop and escalate.

steps.title: Load the firmware from the USB drive
steps.purpose: Install the new firmware version onto the tipper controller.
steps.instruction: Load the firmware from the USB drive.
steps.expected_result: The firmware is loaded from the USB drive.
steps.healthy_condition: The firmware load completes from the USB drive.
steps.failure_condition: The firmware cannot be loaded from the USB drive or the load does not complete.

actions.label: Load firmware
actions.exact_action: Load the firmware from the USB drive.

stop_or_escalate_if: The firmware cannot be loaded from the USB drive.

source_evidence.text: Load the firmware from the USB drive.
If the firmware cannot be loaded from the USB drive, stop and escalate.

steps.title: Reboot the controller normally
steps.purpose: Return the controller to normal operating boot after firmware installation.
steps.instruction: Power the controller off and back on, and allow it to boot normally without pressing any buttons.
steps.expected_result: The controller reboots and begins a normal boot sequence.
steps.healthy_condition: The controller boots normally without requiring button input.
steps.failure_condition: The controller does not boot normally after the power cycle.

actions.label: Power cycle controller
actions.exact_action: Power the controller off and back on, and allow it to boot normally without pressing any buttons.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Expected normal post-boot HMI state after power cycle.
Controller startup and network verification screen context after normal boot.

stop_or_escalate_if: The controller does not boot normally after the power cycle.

source_evidence.text: Power the controller off and back on, and allow it to boot normally without pressing any buttons.
If the controller does not boot normally after the power cycle, stop and escalate.

steps.title: Verify the Visu_Default screen
steps.purpose: Confirm that the controller returned to the expected default HMI screen after reboot.
steps.instruction: Verify that the HMI shows the "Visu_Default" screen displaying the IP and subnet mask settings.
steps.expected_result: The HMI shows the Visu_Default screen with the IP and subnet mask settings.
steps.healthy_condition: The Visu_Default screen is visible and displays the IP and subnet mask settings.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_Default screen does not appear or does not display the IP and subnet mask settings.

actions.label: Check Visu_Default
actions.exact_action: Verify that the HMI shows the "Visu_Default" screen displaying the IP and subnet mask settings.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Visu_Default screen and the displayed IP and subnet mask settings.
Expected post-boot HMI screen state showing network settings.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Visu_Default screen does not appear.
The Visu_Default screen does not display the IP and subnet mask settings.

source_evidence.text: Verify that the HMI shows the "Visu_Default" screen displaying the IP and subnet mask settings.
If the Visu_Default screen does not appear or does not display the IP and subnet mask settings, stop and escalate.

steps.title: Open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen
steps.purpose: Confirm that HMI navigation to the expected operational screen works after firmware installation.
steps.instruction: Press F3 to go to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The HMI changes to the Visu_MCP_Dual screen when F3 is pressed.
steps.failure_condition: F3 does not open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

actions.label: Press F3
actions.exact_action: Press F3 to go to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen reached using F3.
Additional HMI context for the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
Additional HMI context for the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: F3 does not open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

source_evidence.text: Press F3 to go to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen.
If F3 does not open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen, stop and escalate.

success_criteria: The firmware is loaded from the USB drive.
The HMI displays the Visu_Default screen with IP and subnet mask settings.
healthy_conditions: A gray screen appears on the HMI during the startup button sequence.
The firmware load completes from the USB drive.
The controller boots normally after the power cycle.
The Visu_Default screen displays the IP and subnet mask settings.
The Visu_MCP_Dual screen opens when F3 is pressed.
failure_conditions: The gray screen does not appear while holding the top three HMI buttons during power-up.
The firmware cannot be loaded from the USB drive.
The controller does not boot normally after the power cycle.
The Visu_Default screen does not appear or does not display the IP and subnet mask settings.
F3 does not open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the gray screen does not appear while holding the top three HMI buttons during power-up.
Stop and escalate if the firmware cannot be loaded from the USB drive.
Stop and escalate if the controller does not boot normally after the power cycle.
Stop and escalate if the Visu_Default screen does not appear or does not display the IP and subnet mask settings.
Stop and escalate if F3 does not open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Startup button sequence and gray-screen entry context.
Startup/setup screen context related to entering the gray-screen mode.
Visu_Default screen with IP and subnet mask settings after normal boot.
Expected post-boot controller startup and network verification state.
